description: Fix two low-severity issues: (1) dev proxy pending array retains timed-out request references causing a minor memory leak during slow restarts, and (2) SVG icon color validation does not recognize modern CSS rgb() space-separated syntax, allowing saturated colors through inactive icon validation.

userStories.title: Clean up timed-out entries from dev-proxy pending array
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want timed-out pending requests to be removed from the pending array immediately so that dead req/res references do not accumulate during slow dev restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: When a pending request times out in whenReady(), it is removed from the pending array immediately after the timeout fires
The drainPending() function does not execute stale (already-settled) callbacks
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/dev-proxy.ts

The `whenReady` function creates a callback that is pushed into the `pending` array. When a timeout fires (the `onTimeout` path), the callback sets `settled = true` and calls `onTimeout()`, but the callback closure remains in the `pending` array, holding references to the `req`, `res`, or `socket` objects via the `fn` and `onTimeout` closures.

Fix: After the timeout fires (in the timeout handler), remove the callback from the `pending` array. The simplest approach is to filter out settled entries:

```ts
// In the timeout handler, after calling onTimeout():
pending.splice(pending.indexOf(entry), 1);
```

Or assign a reference to the pushed function so it can be removed by reference. Another approach: change `pending` to use a Set for O(1) deletion.

The `drainPending` function already handles the `settled` flag correctly (it checks and skips settled entries), but removing timed-out entries eagerly means `drainPending` processes fewer items and the references are freed sooner.

Note: This is dev-only code. The fix is straightforward and low-risk.

userStories.title: Support modern CSS rgb() syntax in SVG icon color validation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the icon validation to correctly parse modern CSS rgb() syntax so that inactive icons with saturated colors in space-separated format are properly flagged and converted to grayscale.
acceptanceCriteria: parseColor correctly parses rgb(255 0 0), rgb(255 0 0 / 0.5), rgba(255 0 0 / 0.5)
parseColor correctly parses percentage values: rgb(100% 0% 0%), rgb(100% 50% 0% / 0.5)
isAchromaticColor returns false for saturated colors in modern syntax (e.g., rgb(255 0 0))
validateInactiveIconColors flags saturated colors written in modern CSS syntax
generateInactiveIcon converts modern-syntax saturated colors to grayscale
Existing tests for legacy comma-separated syntax continue to pass
New unit tests in validate-icon.test.ts cover all modern syntax variants
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.ts

The `parseColor` function (around line 228) uses a regex to match rgb/rgba values. The current regex only matches the legacy comma-separated syntax: `rgb(R, G, B)` and `rgba(R, G, B, A)`.

Modern CSS Color Level 4 syntax allows:
- Space-separated: `rgb(255 0 0)`, `rgba(255 0 0 / 0.5)`
- Percentage values: `rgb(100% 0% 0%)`, `rgb(50% 50% 50% / 0.8)`
- Mixed: technically `rgb(255 0 0 / 50%)` is valid too

Fix approach: Extend the rgb/rgba regex to also match space-separated syntax, or add a second regex/parsing path. For percentage values, convert to 0-255 range (multiply by 2.55 and round).

A clean approach is to add a second regex for the modern syntax:
```ts
// Modern syntax: rgb(R G B) or rgb(R G B / A)
const modernRgbMatch = value.match(/^rgba?\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+%?)(?:\s*\/\s*[\d.]+%?)?\s*\)$/i);
```

Then parse each component, handling the % suffix by converting to 0-255.

Test file: platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.test.ts
